hero.p1: I'm Ryan - Catholic, husband, and product engineer building India's unlimited internet dream.
hero.p2: From the real world impact of Wiom's decision science engines to incessant aura-farming by sharpening my vim-first open source CLI/TUI stack, I love what I do.
hero.p3: I spend my spare time preparing for the AGI-apocolypse by re-learining high school math and physics with a focus on intuition (instead of rigour), and documenting it all at notes.ryangerardwilson.com

projects.name: vixl
projects.catchLine: Excel is bloated and opaque on large sheets; I wanted fast, home-row spreadsheets.
features: Vim-first TUI spreadsheet editor for CSV/Parquet/XLSX/HDF5 with Pandas syntax.
Handles 100k+ row workflows without leaving the home row.
projects.linkedTools: links with xyz for fast backlog CSV edits.

projects.name: gvim
projects.catchLine: I wanted graph/animation-rich calculus notes without leaving my Vim muscle memory.
features: Made to take graph/animation-heavy calculus notes without bloated software.
GTK4 GUI that preserves Vim navigation across document blocks.
Renders Python graphs and ThreeJS animations inline, stored as git-friendly .gvim files.
projects.linkedTools: pairs with o for vault navigation and vixl for data-driven notes.

projects.name: ai
projects.catchLine: I wanted to study Responses vs Completions and prove a pure CLI aesthetic.
features: Built to study the Responses API vs. Completions using a pure CLI (no TUI).
Rebuilt opencode as a terminal-first assistant for editing, diff audits, Q&A, and repo analysis.
Includes Git safeguards, sandboxed shell commands, and secure API key storage.
projects.linkedTools: Streams reasoning inline while pairing cleanly with your terminal + Vim stack.

projects.name: o
projects.catchLine: File managers force arrows/mouse; I wanted hjkl-only navigation for the filesystem.
features: Built to eliminate arrow-key/mouse strain with Vim's hjkl-only navigation.
Python curses file browser with modal nav, yank/cut/paste/delete, and picker modes.
Matrix-inspired rain display keeps context while you move fast.
projects.linkedTools: opens vixl and rt directly from directory view.

projects.name: xyz
bootLog: ready // log when x -> y so z
projects.catchLine: To-dos reward busywork; I wanted JTBD outcomes that compound progress.
features: Built to force JTBD outcomes instead of busywork checklists.
Vim-first terminal task tracker with North Star scores (p/q/r) for value, impact, embodiment.
Pure Python (curses + CSV) with instant setup for clarity-driven creators.
projects.linkedTools: exports tasks to vixl for deeper analysis.

projects.name: rt
projects.catchLine: AI code slop dulls intuition; I wanted drills that embed expert patterns.
features: Built to counter AI code slop by drilling expert snippets into muscle memory.
CLI tutor with rote/jump modes, 90%+ accuracy gates, and in-place editing.
Parses .md courses into structured lessons with bookmarks and search.
projects.linkedTools: pairs with o for editing lessons in place.

projects.name: clipai
projects.catchLine: I wanted AI answers without context switching, triggered straight from the clipboard.
features: Clipboard watcher swaps ai{{ prompt }} with OpenAI responses in real time.
CLI mode pipes answers straight into your buffer for scripts and automations.
Wayland-native service built on wl-clipboard with a systemd user unit for persistence.
projects.linkedTools: feeds vixl, o, rt, and xyz without breaking flow.

projects.name: gmail
projects.catchLine: Gmail's web UI drags simple mail into browser fog; I wanted multi-account email flows I could declare once and drive from the shell.
features: Declarative Gmail CLI with preset-based accounts, automatic signatures, token storage, and timezone-aware timestamps.
Handles send, reply, thread open, attachments, contacts, spam cleanup, starred mail, and interactive unread/read audits without leaving the terminal.
Uses Gmail OAuth scopes for send/read/modify instead of brittle scraping.
projects.linkedTools: shares the same editor-first compose instinct as slack, whatsapp, x, and linkedin.

projects.name: audio
projects.catchLine: Linux audio settings are click-heavy nonsense; I wanted pactl-driven sink switching from the terminal.
features: CLI for listing and switching PulseAudio/PipeWire output devices with direct sink and sink:port targeting.
Supports interactive selection, fast `--list` inspection, and explicit device jumps for headphones vs speakers.
Keeps Bluetooth pairing manual through `bluetoothctl`, then hands device switching back to one clean command.
projects.linkedTools: pairs naturally with rlc when I want music moving between headphones, desk speakers, and Bluetooth boxes fast.

projects.name: rlc
projects.catchLine: GUI music players feel mushy and overlit; I wanted local playback, ffplay speed, and an ASCII visualizer in the terminal.
features: Curses-based local music player with keyboard-first controls, playlist reordering, search, shuffle, and command bar actions.
Uses `ffmpeg`/`ffplay` for playback and rendering, plus `yt-dlp` for pulling YouTube audio straight into the music directory.
Stores only a small XDG config for music dir and FPS, then gets out of the way.
projects.linkedTools: teams up with audio so output switching stays as keyboard-native as playback.

projects.name: blog
projects.catchLine: Posting clips across platforms was repetitive ceremony; I wanted one CLI to record, trim, caption, and publish to X plus LinkedIn.
features: Publishes text or media to multiple platforms from one command, with `$EDITOR` compose mode and XDG-configured per-platform argument templates.
Includes built-in screen recording, stop-and-trim flow, local test renders, sync diagnostics, and recorder cache cleanup.
Treats x and linkedin as composable backends instead of making me retype the same post twice.
projects.linkedTools: sits above x and linkedin when I want a publish-once workflow instead of per-platform babysitting.

projects.name: x
projects.catchLine: X is useful and distractingly awful at the same time; I wanted a CLI that posts text or media without dragging me into the feed.
features: Minimal command-line publisher with Vim compose mode, media attach support, and automatic 280-character guardrails.
Uses OAuth2 PKCE user login by default, with OAuth1 fallback for text-only posts when needed.
Built around the official Python XDK for v2 media upload and post creation.
projects.linkedTools: powers blog's cross-post flow and mirrors linkedin's editor-first posting ergonomics.

projects.name: linkedin
projects.catchLine: LinkedIn punishes brevity with layers of form fields and recruiter varnish; I wanted a small CLI that just posts the thing.
features: Posts text, images, or video from the command line with Vim compose mode and a direct `main.py` flow.
Handles OAuth token storage at `~/.linkedin/oauth2_token.json` and can auto-start login when no valid token exists.
Waits for LinkedIn media asset processing before creating the post so uploads do not race and fail halfway.
projects.linkedTools: pairs with x directly and plugs into blog when I want one caption pushed to both feeds.

projects.name: slack
projects.catchLine: Slack is fine until I need one fast direct message; I wanted a CLI that messages people as me without tab-hopping.
features: Sends Slack DMs from the terminal using a user token, addressed by user ID, email, or saved labels.
Supports `$EDITOR` compose mode plus CLI label creation for repeat contacts.
Keeps the permissions narrow: `chat:write`, `im:write`, and `users:read` only when email lookup is needed.
projects.linkedTools: shares the same label-driven, editor-first messaging posture as whatsapp and gmail.

projects.name: whatsapp
projects.catchLine: WhatsApp Web makes a two-line message feel like a small pilgrimage; I wanted one command and done.
features: Minimal CLI that drives WhatsApp Web through Playwright, with persisted login stored in `~/.whatsapp-web` after the first QR scan.
Supports direct numbers, contact labels in XDG config, profile clearing, and send timeouts for flaky sessions.
Installer sets up a private virtualenv plus Playwright browser binaries so the runtime stays boxed in.
projects.linkedTools: fits beside slack and gmail for outbound messaging when I refuse to live in browser tabs.

meta.label: > focus
meta.body: Productionalizing AI/ML models via agentic orchestration. Terminal-native product design.

meta.label: > experience
meta.body: 10+ years of polyglot experience across dev, data science, and intellectual property.

meta.label: > current_role
meta.body: Entrepreneur-in-Residence @ Wiom, building unlimited internet for India.

entries.headline: Married my beautiful wife
bullets: Married my wife – very beautiful indeed.
Relearned high-school math while shipping md2ltx, GitGuru, and DataSling.
Explored FP vs OOP, built DeathStarPilot, reaffirmed a vim-first workflow.
Rebuilt personal infra, consolidated automation, delivered Wiom's Genie engine.
Created rt and companion tutor apps to keep coding taste sharp.

entries.headline: Entrepreneurial rollercoaster to Wiom
bullets: Built LegalTech apps, burned savings, learned from a startup crash.
Joined Wiom's mission; shipped HAPPY and GENIE.
Deepened craft in Bash, PHP, Python, JS, and Gospel piano.
Explored Rust for a year before returning to Python's pragmatism.
Proved build-in-production with Python, JS, and SQLite for Wiom's internal CRM.

entries.headline: Law, code, and rediscovering craft
bullets: Rode out COVID disruption while practicing law.
Learned PHP; rekindled piano disciplines and builder mindset.

entries.headline: IP and tech attorney years
bullets: Worked as an IP and technology lawyer, observing how labor-driven businesses scale.

entries.headline: First LegalTech startup crash
bullets: Joined a LegalTech startup.
Ran email marketing campaigns; learned basic HTML.
Fell in love with the idea that computers can do whatever you tell them to—provided you ask nicely.

entries.headline: Law school foundations
bullets: Joined RMLNLU in Lucknow, grounding discipline in dialect and rhetoric.

entries.headline: Dropping out to chase better questions
bullets: Walked away from DU commerce to find deeper learning paths.

entries.headline: Challenging the script
bullets: Silent-quit CBSE science, refusing assembly-line thinking.

entries.headline: Dreaming in chords and code
bullets: Imagined life as a mad scientist, Liverpool midfielder, and pianist.

entries.headline: And so it began
bullets: Baptized Catholic.
